differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with lupus nephritis
Primary Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS) with catastrophic presentation
Membranous Nephropathy with secondary hypercoagulability (renal vein thrombosis)
Infective endocarditis with septic pulmonary and renal emboli
Primary nephrotic syndrome (e.g., Focal Segmental Glomerulosclerosis) complicated by venous thromboembolism
next_steps: Comprehensive autoimmune serology (ANA, dsDNA, complement levels, ANCA, anti-GBM, anti-phospholipid antibodies)
Echocardiogram (transthoracic or transesophageal) to evaluate for vegetations and right heart strain
Blood and sputum cultures (if not already finalized)
Anticoagulation therapy (starting with therapeutic dose heparin/LMWH)
Renal biopsy (once the patient is hemodynamically stable)
Renal ultrasound with Doppler to monitor renal vein thrombosis status
Aggressive infectious disease coverage pending culture results
